summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering bill payments, a fire department equipment grant for pagers and radios, a no-parking resolution for Marsh Circle, endorsement of a living history event at Cook Cabin, and several personnel actions following an executive session. Key personnel decisions included rehiring a part-time firefighter/paramedic, hiring a full-time police officer, and promoting an officer to sergeant.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for March 25 through April 8, 2025
Approved increase in revenue and appropriations for Ohio State Fire Marshal grant for pagers and radios
Approved increase in appropriations of $14,898.93 for 2025 equipment grant (2193-220-420-0008)
Approved Resolution 005-2025 establishing a no-parking area on the north side of Marsh Circle
Approved endorsement of the 1840 Living History Camp at Cook Cabin on April 26
Approved adjournment into executive session at 6:16 PM under ORC 121.22(G)(1)
Approved rehire of part-time firefighter/paramedic Robert Coy Webster at $22.50/hour effective April 20, 2025
Approved hiring of Garrett Vanover as full-time police officer contingent upon pre-employment screening
Approved promotion of Officer James McFarland to police sergeant effective May 4, 2025
action_items: Trustees to decide on Grand Marshal for upcoming Memorial Day parade by April 22 meeting
Trustees to decide on speaker for Memorial Day service on May 26
Bob Meadows (Service Director) to coordinate sending a truck and crew to assist Village of Richmond with flood cleanup when waters recede
Administrator Rose to post 1840 Living History Camp event on Facebook page and digital sign
Administrator Rose to complete pre-employment screening process for Garrett Vanover
Bob Meadows to continue monitoring Timber Ridge subdivision fiber optic/trunk line issue with Jay Daniels
key_amounts: $14,898.93 - Ohio State Fire Marshal grant for pagers and radios (equipment grant)
$22.50/hour - pay rate for rehired part-time firefighter/paramedic Robert Coy Webster
70 feet - no-parking distance established on Marsh Circle (47 feet from stop sign to tree for visibility)
notable_topics: Condolences to the Hauserman family for loss of a loved one
Offer to assist Village of Richmond with flood cleanup
Timber Ridge subdivision fiber optic trunk line damage from 2022 boring
CCTA special meeting with state representatives Schmidt and Adam Burke on pending legislation
Ohio State Fire Marshal grant for pagers and radios
No-parking resolution for Marsh Circle in Woodland Streams neighborhood due to visibility issues
Endorsement of 1840 Living History Camp at Cook Cabin
Hiring of full-time police officer to fill last vacancy
Promotion of Officer McFarland to police sergeant
attendees: Dennis Cooper (Washington Township Trustee, mentioned via email)
notes: Meeting included an executive session from approximately 6:16 PM to 7:08 PM for personnel matters. Trustee Hodges noted personal significance in the McFarland promotion motion, stating his father was the one who originally hired James McFarland. Transcript had significant garbled/corrupted sections likely due to automated transcription errors. The Clermont County Township Association (CCTA) held a special meeting the previous night with Ohio Township Association representative Kyle and state Representatives Schmidt and Adam Burke discussing nine legislative bills.
title: BOT 4-8-25
